---
title: "Quarto 1 — Your First Report"
subtitle: "Continue one cumulative learner document"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Where we are

::: {.incremental}
- You knitted one R Markdown report.
- You translated a safe copy to Quarto.
- Now continue `student_work/my_report.qmd`.
- The workshop website remains read-only.
:::

# The familiar model {.section-divider}

## What transfers

YAML + Markdown prose + executable chunks still produce the document.

::: {.incremental}
- `.qmd` source
- `format:` in YAML
- `#|` chunk-option lines
- **Render** instead of **Knit**
:::

## A useful HTML header

```yaml
---
title: "Biomarker Summary"
author: "Your name"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
---
```

::: {.fragment}
Start with one reliable format. Demonstrate DOCX later.
:::

# Quarto additions {.section-divider}

## Callouts

```markdown
::: {.callout-note}
These data are synthetic and are used only for training.
:::
```

::: {.callout-note}
Callouts make cautions, assumptions, and decisions visible without custom HTML.
:::

## Chunk options

```` markdown
```{r}`r ''`
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

dat <- read.csv("data/workshop_biomarkers.csv")
```
````

::: {.fragment}
Option names use hyphens; YAML-style booleans use lowercase.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Add to the cumulative report

In `student_work/my_report.qmd`:

1. Add your title and name.
2. Enable a table of contents and code folding.
3. Customize the synthetic-data callout.
4. Render from a restarted R session.

Use `student_work/checkpoints/01_yaml_complete.qmd` only for recovery.

::: {.callout-important}
Success: the learner report renders without involving another course file.
:::
